title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine powered by sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices enabling auditable, evidence-backed hiring decisions.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California ZIP codes using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM trained on historical real estate transaction data. The Streamlit-powered interface allows users to search by location and input property characteristics to generate price estimates, with results visualized through interactive Plotly charts. Model training leveraged scikit-learn pipelines with feature engineering on socioeconomic and property-level variables to optimize predictive accuracy across diverse California housing markets.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, with threshold tuning to maximize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, revealing conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to collect, process, and visualize datasets through HTTP requests and structured data manipulation with Pandas and NumPy. Applied statistical methods and created informative visualizations using Matplotlib and Seaborn to uncover patterns and trends within the data. The project demonstrated end-to-end data science workflow from raw data acquisition to actionable visual insights presented in a Jupyter Notebook environment.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, pandas, and NumPy to process and evaluate network or behavioral data fetched via the Requests library. The project reconstructed the core logic of an automated pipeline by hand, enabling step-by-step inspection of how latent or dormant patterns are identified within the dataset. This approach served as a transparent reference implementation for validating and understanding the underlying detection methodology.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack AI roster building assistant for fantasy football that integrates with the public Sleeper API to display a user's leagues, rosters, and available players through a React + Vite frontend. Leveraged Python with pandas and NumPy in Jupyter Notebooks to process player and roster data, enabling data-driven roster recommendations. The prototype demonstrates end-to-end data flow from live API calls to an interactive browser-based interface, providing fantasy football players with actionable lineup insights.

title: DSC 80 Project Notebook
description: Built a data science pipeline using pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn to clean, process, and model a structured dataset, addressing missing data and engineering features to prepare inputs for machine learning. Applied classification or regression models to generate predictions, evaluating performance through cross-validation and relevant metrics. Visualized key patterns and model outcomes using Matplotlib and Plotly to communicate findings clearly.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Mapped bike lane usage and traffic volume data onto a dynamic interface, allowing users to filter and examine cycling trends across different streets and neighborhoods. The project revealed spatial patterns in cyclist behavior and infrastructure utilization throughout the city.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This project served as a hands-on introduction to Git version control and GitHub Desktop, demonstrating the workflow of editing files, tracking changes, and committing updates to a repository. The exercise involved modifying a README file and using GitHub Desktop to stage and commit the changes, establishing familiarity with core version control concepts. The result was a functional local repository connected to GitHub, with a documented commit history reflecting the file update.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
